name: Db

unions.name: Db
unions.comment: Short for "Database", it stores data by unique identifiers

aliases.name: Row
aliases.comment: A single row in the `Db`; an `item` paired with its `Id`

values.name: allPresent
values.comment:
Verify that all the items are present, and if not, fail with a list of the items that are missing

    allPresent [ (id1, Just user1), (id2, Just user2) ]
    --> Ok [ (id1, user1), (id2, user2) ]

    allPresent [ (id1, Nothing), (id2, Just user2), (id3, Nothing) ]
    --> Err [ id1, id3 ]
values.type: List.List ( Id.Id item, Maybe.Maybe item ) -> Result.Result (List.List (Id.Id item)) (List.List (Db.Row item))

values.name: empty
values.comment: An empty `Db` with no entries
values.type: Db.Db item

values.name: filter
values.comment: Filter out items from a `Db`
values.type: (Db.Row item -> Basics.Bool) -> Db.Db item -> Db.Db item

values.name: filterMissing
values.comment:
Take a `List` of items, presumably ones you just retrieved using `getMany`, and filter out
the ones that werent present in the `Db`

    myFriendsList
        |> Db.getMany people
        |> Db.filterMissing
        -->: List (Row Person)
values.type: List.List ( Id.Id item, Maybe.Maybe item ) -> List.List (Db.Row item)

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Initialize a `Db` from a list of id-value pairs
values.type: List.List (Db.Row item) -> Db.Db item

values.name: get
values.comment: Get the item under the provided `Id`
values.type: Db.Db item -> Id.Id item -> Maybe.Maybe item

values.name: getMany
values.comment: Get many items from a `Db`. The `(id, Nothing)` case represents the item under that `Id` being absent.
values.type: Db.Db item -> List.List (Id.Id item) -> List.List ( Id.Id item, Maybe.Maybe item )

values.name: getWithId
values.comment: Just like `get`, except it comes with the `Id`, for those cases where you dont want the item separated from its `Id`
values.type: Db.Db item -> Id.Id item -> ( Id.Id item, Maybe.Maybe item )

values.name: insert
values.comment: Insert an item into the `Db` under the given `Id`
values.type: Db.Row item -> Db.Db item -> Db.Db item

values.name: insertMany
values.comment: Insert many items into the `Db` under their given `Id`s
values.type: List.List (Db.Row item) -> Db.Db item -> Db.Db item

values.name: map
values.comment: Map a `Db` to a different data type.
values.type: (a -> b) -> Db.Db a -> Db.Db b

values.name: mapItem
values.comment: Apply a change to just one item in the `Db`, assuming the item is in the `Db` in the first place. This function is just like `update` except deleting the item is not possible.
values.type: Id.Id item -> (item -> item) -> Db.Db item -> Db.Db item

values.name: remove
values.comment: Remove the item at the given `Id`, if it exists
values.type: Id.Id item -> Db.Db item -> Db.Db item

values.name: toList
values.comment: Turn your `Db` into a list
values.type: Db.Db item -> List.List (Db.Row item)

values.name: update
values.comment: Update an item in a `Db`, using an update function. If the item doesnt exist in the `Db`, it comes into the update as `Nothing`. If a `Nothing` comes out of the update function, the value under that id will be removed.
values.type: Id.Id item -> (Maybe.Maybe item -> Maybe.Maybe item) -> Db.Db item -> Db.Db item

name: Id

values.name: decoder
values.comment:
Decode an `Id`

    Decode.decodeString (Decode.field "id" Id.decoder) "{\"id\":\"19\"}"
    -- Ok (Id "19") : Result String Id
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder (Id.Id x)

values.name: encode
values.comment:
Encode an `Id`

    Encode.encode 0 (Id.encode id)
    -- ""hDFL0Cs2EqWJ4jc3kMtOrKdEUTWh"" : String

    [ ("id", Id.encode id) ]
        |> Encode.object
        |> Encode.encode 0

    -- {\"id\":\"hDFL0Cs2EqWJ4jc3kMtOrKdEUTWh\"} : String
values.type: Id.Id x -> Json.Encode.Value

values.name: fromString
values.comment:
Make an id from a string

    Id.fromString "vq93rUv0A4"
values.type: String.String -> Id.Id x

values.name: generator
values.type: Random.Generator (Id.Id x)

values.name: toString
values.comment: Extract the string from an id.
values.type: Id.Id x -> String.String

name: Remote.Db

unions.name: Db
unions.comment: Short for "Database", it stores data by unique identifiers. The `error` is for loading errors, such as if an http request fails.

aliases.name: Row
aliases.comment: A single row in the `Db`; an `Id`, corresponding to either an `item` or the `error` that happend when attempting to load it.
aliases.type: ( Remote.Id.Id error item, RemoteData.RemoteData error item )

values.name: empty
values.comment: An empty `Db` with no entries
values.type: Remote.Db.Db error item

values.name: fail
values.comment: When data fails to load, set it as `Failure` in the `Db`
values.type: ( Remote.Id.Id error item, error ) -> Remote.Db.Db error item -> Remote.Db.Db error item

values.name: failMany
values.comment: Fail many `Id`, representing them as failing to load in the `Db`
values.type: List.List ( Remote.Id.Id error item, error ) -> Remote.Db.Db error item -> Remote.Db.Db error item

values.name: get
values.comment: Get an item out of a `Db`. Notice how it does not return a `Maybe` like `Dict` do. An `Id` that is not present in the `Db` is returned as `RemoteData.NotAsked`.
values.type: Remote.Db.Db error item -> Remote.Id.Id error item -> RemoteData.RemoteData error item

values.name: getMany
values.comment: Get many items from a `Db`. The `(id, RemoteData.NotAsked)` case represents the item under that `Id` being absent.
values.type: Remote.Db.Db error item -> List.List (Remote.Id.Id error item) -> List.List (Remote.Db.Row error item)

values.name: getWithId
values.comment: Just like `get`, except it comes with the `Id`, for those cases where you dont want the item separated from its `Id`
values.type: Remote.Db.Db error item -> Remote.Id.Id error item -> Remote.Db.Row error item

values.name: insert
values.comment:
A general insert function into `Db`. You can insert a `RemoteData error item` into a `Db`

    -- if the id is not in the `Db`
    Remote.Db.get db id
    --> RemoteData.NotAsked

    -- but if it is..
    Remote.Db.get
        (Remote.Db.insert (id, RemoteData.Failure "Could not load") db)
        id
    --> RemoteData.Failure "Could not load"
values.type: Remote.Db.Row error item -> Remote.Db.Db error item -> Remote.Db.Db error item

values.name: loading
values.comment:
Mark the `Id` as `Loading`. After this function is used on a `Db`, it will return `Loading` for that `Id`

    -- if the id is not in the `Db`
    Remote.Db.get db id
    --> RemoteData.NotAsked

    -- but if it is..
    Remote.Db.get (Remote.Db.loading id db) id
    --> RemoteData.Loading
values.type: Remote.Id.Id error item -> Remote.Db.Db error item -> Remote.Db.Db error item

values.name: loadingMany
values.comment: Mark a `List` of `Id` as `Loading` in a `Db`
values.type: List.List (Remote.Id.Id error item) -> Remote.Db.Db error item -> Remote.Db.Db error item

values.name: map
values.comment: Map a `Db` to a different data type.
values.type: (a -> b) -> Remote.Db.Db error a -> Remote.Db.Db error b

values.name: mapError
values.comment: Map the error type of a `Db` to something else.
values.type: (a -> b) -> Remote.Db.Db a item -> Remote.Db.Db b item

values.name: mapItem
values.comment: Apply a change to just one item in the `Db`, assuming the item is in the `Db` in the first place. This function is just like `update` except deleting the item is not possible.
values.type: Remote.Id.Id error item -> (item -> item) -> Remote.Db.Db error item -> Remote.Db.Db error item

values.name: remove
values.comment:
Simply remove an `Id` from a `Db`, resetting that item to `RemoteData.NotAsked`

    Remote.Db.get (Remote.Db.remove id db) id
    --> RemoteData.NotAsked
values.type: Remote.Id.Id error item -> Remote.Db.Db error item -> Remote.Db.Db error item

values.name: succeed
values.comment: Insert an `item` into a `Db`, when it has been successfully loaded
values.type: ( Remote.Id.Id error item, item ) -> Remote.Db.Db error item -> Remote.Db.Db error item

values.name: succeedMany
values.comment: Insert many `item`s into a `Db`, such as when you loaded many items successfully.
values.type: List.List ( Remote.Id.Id error item, item ) -> Remote.Db.Db error item -> Remote.Db.Db error item

values.name: update
values.comment: Update an item in a `Db`, using an update function. If the item doesnt exist in the `Db`, it comes into the update as `RemoteData.NotAsked`. If a `RemoteData.NotAsked` comes out of the update function, the value under that id will be removed.
values.type: Remote.Id.Id error item -> (RemoteData.RemoteData error item -> RemoteData.RemoteData error item) -> Remote.Db.Db error item -> Remote.Db.Db error item
